category_title: Nonverbal students with ASD using iPads for communication and academic tasks
minimal_context: The student is using an iPad with an AAC app during snack-time to request food.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is snack time. Please use your iPad to request what you want to eat.

STUDENT: (Sits with the iPad open to the AAC app but does not fulfill the app function to request a snack.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and tell me what you want.

STUDENT: (Navigates away from the AAC app and goes to the homescreen.)

TUTOR: Keep trying.

STUDENT: (Waits 3 seconds on the homescreen and then intentionally selects Doodle Buddy, violating the AAC app function during independent use.)

options.A: Create only rules that are absolutely necessary for maintaining a safe environment; if staying on the AAC app is not critical, do not require it.
options.B: Provide the student with two separate iPads, dedicating one exclusively for the AAC communication app and the other for games and entertainment.
options.C: Publicly celebrate awards and improvements, such as reduced app violations or consistent AAC usage, as often as possible during the snack-time routine.
options.D: Model appropriate behaviors of caring and cooperation; reflect support, acceptance, and positive regard while redirecting the student's off-task app usage during snack-time.
